title: Verify/adapt UI under mandatory Liquid Glass (Xcode 27 SDK)
description:
Building MacDown against the macOS 27 SDK auto-adopts Liquid Glass — the opt-out (UIDesignRequiresCompatibility) is removed in Xcode 27, so this lands silently on the first 27-SDK recompile whether or not we opt in. Need to recompile and verify the AppKit UI under the new material, then fix breakage.

## Context
MacDown is Objective-C/AppKit. Custom toolbar backgrounds and any hard-coded window/nav background coloring are the likely break points. Source from WWDC 2026 review (apple-features.md §5, §9). Highest time-sensitivity of the WWDC items because it cannot be opted out of.
acceptance_criteria: App recompiled against macOS 27 SDK; toolbar (MPToolbarController), MPDocumentSplitView, MPEditorView, and window chrome render correctly under Liquid Glass; custom nav/tab/toolbar background colors that fight the glass material removed or adapted; verified against the user-facing transparency slider.

title: WKWebView Stage 6: finalize + delete legacy code
description: Confirm zoom/print. Delete DOMNode+Text.{h,m}, WebView+WebViewPrivateHeaders.h, MPMathJaxListener.{h,m}, the MathJax redirect, the WebView (Shortcut) category, legacy Web*Delegate conformances, kMPMathJaxCDN, unused JavaScriptCore import. Drop deleted files from the Xcode target. Clean build, zero legacy WebView references.
close_reason: Deleted dead legacy-WebView code: DOMNode+Text.{h,m}, WebView+WebViewPrivateHeaders.h, MPMathJaxListener.{h,m} (all zero call sites), their 14 pbxproj entries, and the #if 0 DOMDocument/DOMNode block in MPDocument.m. Clean build, 53/53 tests pass, zero compiled legacy WebView references. DEVIATIONS from bead text (verified against code): kMPMathJaxCDN is KEPT (still loads MathJax from CDN in mathjaxScripts); the JavaScriptCore import is KEPT (active JSContext use in MPUtilities.m) -- neither is 'unused'; the 'WebView (Shortcut) category' and 'Web*Delegate conformances' don't exist (already removed in earlier stages); the MathJax redirect was already gone. Zoom/print unaffected -- both run through self.preview (WKWebView) APIs untouched here.

title: WKWebView Stage 5: scroll-sync rebuild (async metrics)
description: Editor side of updateHeaderLocations stays sync; preview header tops + cached scrollHeight/innerHeight fetched async via injected JS. Drive preview via fire-and-forget window.scrollTo. Warm cache on willStartLiveScroll:.
notes:
From PR #5 review (CodeRabbit/Codex): make MathJax completion load-exact. init.js posts a constant sentinel (postMessage(1)), so a stale mathJaxEnd from a superseded load can satisfy the next load's completion guard early. Fix as part of the scroll-sync rebuild: inject a per-load token (e.g. a per-load WKUserScript at documentStart setting window.__mpLoadToken=N, or evaluateJavaScript), have init.js post that token, and validate it in -userContentController:didReceiveScriptMessage: against the current generation before latching completion. Currently inert (scroll-sync stubbed; every load's completion incl. the 3s fallback re-enables window flush, so no permanent freeze).
Update (PR #5 round 2): added a previewNavigationFinished gate on the mathJaxEnd handler — a stale message arriving during the next load's provisional phase (navFinished=NO) is now rejected, which eliminates the freeze risk. Residual: a stale message arriving in the narrow window between the new load's didFinish and its own mathJaxEnd can still complete it slightly early (inert while scroll-sync stubbed). The full per-load token (host-injected, validated in the handler) closes that last window if the scroll-sync rebuild needs exact timing.
RESOLVED in stage 3 (PR #5 round 4): the per-load token is now implemented. didProduceHTMLOutput bumps previewLoadGeneration and injects window.__mpLoadToken via a documentStart WKUserScript; init.js echoes it on mathJaxEnd; the handler rejects messages whose token != current generation. So scroll-sync (.5.4) no longer needs to build per-load completion identity — it can rely on the existing exactly-once completion firing after MathJax for the correct load. Token round-trip runtime-verified (token==gen on every message).
close_reason: Scroll-sync rebuilt for WKWebView (async metrics + window.scrollTo); runtime-verified bidirectional tracking

title: WKWebView Stage 4: async word count + divider background color
description: Port DOMNode+Text tree walk to injected JS (Intl.Segmenter; preserve PRE>CODE=0 and inline-code=1 rules; diff against old counts). updateWordCount async via evaluateJavaScript:completionHandler:. Cache body background color, refresh on load; redrawDivider reads cache.
close_reason: Ported DOMNode+Text walk to injected JS (Intl.Segmenter, PRE>CODE=0/inline-code=1 preserved, runtime-verified words=4/chars=32/noSpace=29); updateWordCount async via evaluateJavaScript; cached body background color refreshed on load, redrawDivider reads cache. Local code-review fixes (weak-self) applied. 45/45 tests pass.

title: WKWebView Stage 3: MathJax completion via WKScriptMessageHandler
description: Replace windowScriptObject MathJaxListener injection with WKScriptMessageHandler + WKUserScript at config time. init.js posts window.webkit.messageHandlers.mathJaxEnd.postMessage(1). Completion handler runs exactly once per load (per-load token) whether MathJax is on or off.
close_reason: MathJax typeset-complete wired through WKScriptMessageHandler (weak wrapper, exactly-once via per-load generation/flag, reset at didStartProvisional, -close teardown). init.js posts mathJaxEnd. Stock CDN MathJax 2.7.3 fires 'End', so the #548 bundled-patch workaround is unneeded. Runtime-verified exactly-once; 45 tests pass.

title: WKWebView Stage 1: scaffolding + asset loading + navigation
description: Replace legacy WebView with a programmatically-created WKWebView. New MPAssetSchemeHandler (WKURLSchemeHandler) serves app-bundle + Application Support assets over macdown-asset: with a path-security gate. MPAsset/MPRenderer emit macdown-asset: URLs for FullLink bundle/AppSupport assets (Embedded export path untouched); MathJax preview served from bundle via the scheme (drops CDN+redirect for preview). Load via loadHTMLString:baseURL: with doc-dir baseURL for relative images (verify on 12.0; fallback macdown-doc: scheme). WKNavigationDelegate replaces WebFrameLoadDelegate+WebPolicyDelegate. Keep zoom (pageZoom) and print (printOperationWithPrintInfo:) working. Temporarily stub word count, scroll-sync, divider color, copy-as-HTML (each tracked by a later-stage bead).
notes: Correction: MathJax preview is NOT served from the bundle in Stage 1. The bundle ships only a patched MathJax.js (for issue #548) that the legacy WebResourceLoadDelegate swapped in; WKWebView has no equivalent, so MathJax loads from the CDN as before. Reinstating the patch / offline bundling is macdown-8tk.5.2.
close_reason: WKWebView scaffolding + macdown-asset scheme handler + navigation landed and verified (CSS, Prism, mermaid, MathJax, relative images, tables render; editorOnRight + link-cancel review fixes confirmed). Later stages tracked by sibling beads; drop-prevention .5.6 and off-main-thread reads .5.7 filed from review.

title: Migrate preview from legacy WebView to WKWebView
description: The big architectural migration. ~60 deprecation warnings in Document/MPDocument.m and Extension/DOMNode+Text.m (WebView, WebFrame, Web*Delegate, DOMDocument/DOMNode). WKWebView's async, process-isolated model changes how the preview receives HTML and how the editor reads the rendered DOM. Subsumes all WebView/DOM/WebFrame deprecation cleanup (so issue A excludes those). Will likely be broken into sub-issues.
close_reason: WKWebView migration complete: all stages (1-6) and child bugs closed. Preview runs entirely on WKWebView, legacy WebView/DOM code deleted (8tk.5.5), scroll-sync rebuilt and the reference-node asymmetry fixed (4y8, PR #12, runtime-verified). Remaining scroll-sync edge cases tracked in macdown-y9j.

title: Revive MacDown on a modern toolchain (Xcode 27+)
description: Umbrella for bringing the ~5-year-dormant codebase up to date. PR #1 (merged) got it building on Xcode 27 with a macOS 12 floor. Remaining modernization work is tracked as children. Prose roadmap lives in CLAUDE.md 'Revival notes'.

title: Validate & sanitize override values from untrusted layers (type + safe vocabulary)
description:
When folder/.macdown.yml and front-matter layers populate overrides (Phase 3), the VALUES come from untrusted input and must be validated/coerced before they reach MPDocumentPreferences's typed getters or path-resolving code. Two requirements, both surfaced by Codex on PR #28:

1. TYPE validation/coercion: a value must match the schema descriptor's type. The YAML/front-matter parser renders all scalars as NSString, so e.g. a bool/int key arrives as a string and must be coerced; a collection (array/dict) for a scalar key, or a number for a string key, must be rejected -- otherwise the generated getter sends boolValue/integerValue to an incompatible object (crash) or hands a non-string to rendering. Decide the contract: the reader coerces per schema type before setOverrideValue:, and/or setOverrideValue: validates against MPSettingDescriptor.type.

2. SAFE VALUE VOCABULARY (security): path-resolved name settings -- htmlStyleName and htmlHighlightingThemeName -- are turned into filesystem paths by MPStylePathForName / theme lookup and embedded by HTML export (MPReadFileOfPath). An untrusted document setting htmlStyleName to '../../outside/secret.css' is a path-traversal / file-exfiltration vector. These keys must be constrained to a safe vocabulary (a bare identifier with no path separators, or a known existing style id) before being honored from folder/document layers. Consider a schema 'valueConstraint' attribute (e.g. identifier-only) alongside the existing security flag (cf. siy.1's read-side consent model). This is the same untrusted-data-escalation threat class as htmlAssetLocalAccessScope, applied to style names.

Must be enforced before htmlStyleName / htmlHighlightingThemeName are actually populated from untrusted layers.

title: Decide Intel vs Apple-silicon-only for distribution (macOS 27 / Xcode 27)
description:
macOS 27 'Golden Gate' drops all Intel Macs (Apple-silicon only; macOS 26 Tahoe was the last Intel-compatible release) and Xcode 27 itself is Apple-silicon only. This doesn't break our code, but it's a deliberate distribution/policy decision we should make rather than drift into.

## Context
Affects Tools/build_for_release.py archive/build settings and the three project targets' MACOSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET. Distribution is Developer ID + notarization + Sparkle (not the Mac App Store), so this is purely our own call. Source: apple-features.md §1, §8, §9. Lower urgency than the Liquid Glass item — no forced change, just a choice to make consciously.
acceptance_criteria: Decision recorded on: (a) whether MacDown's release artifacts stay universal (x86_64+arm64) or go arm64-only, and (b) whether to raise the MACOSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET floor (currently 12.0). Tools/build_for_release.py and the deployment-target settings updated to match the decision.

title: Render data: URI images in WKWebView preview
description: Phase 2. data: URI images do not render in the WKWebView preview (confirmed 2026-06-17: a 200x200 data:image/png inserted correctly but showed nothing while a heading rendered - pipeline is live). NOT caused by drag-drop insertion, the file://->asset rewriter (matches file: only), or hoedown escaping (data: chars are all HREF_SAFE; URL passes intact). Root cause is the WKWebView preview layer: loadHTMLString:baseURL: with a custom asset-scheme base URL + a data: image. Fallout from the WKWebView migration (macdown-8tk.5).

title: Settings resolution engine (effectiveValue), app-layer first
description: Phase 0. Single resolver: effectiveValue(forKey:, for:document) walking app defaults -> .macdown.yml chain -> front matter, last-writer-wins. Initially app-layer only so feature code reads through it immediately; later phases fill in directory/front-matter layers behind the same interface. Everything reads via this one point - no ad-hoc per-feature lookups.
close_reason: Closed

title: Settings schema + safe-subset allowlist (overridable keys)
description: Phase 0. Define the overridable-settings schema shared by ALL layers (app prefs, .macdown.yml, front matter): key names, types, value vocab, and the safe-subset allowlist. Security: keys that widen local file access (htmlAssetLocalAccessScope) or load active/remote content (themes/CSS/JS) are app-only - never settable from directory configs or front matter (untrusted-folder escalation). Generalizes macdown-2sd's which-prefs question. In front matter, namespace under a macdown: mapping to avoid colliding with users own keys; .macdown.yml is flat.
design:
DECIDED 2026-06-17 (with Stuart).

Each setting in the schema carries THREE attributes, not a single scope enum:

- readableFrom: which layers may supply a value — {app, folder, document}.
- appWritable: whether automated settings-writing machinery may persist it on
  the user's behalf — {yes (normal consent) | explicit-only | no}.
- security: marks settings whose override WIDENS a capability; subject to the
  read-side asymmetry below.

Two distinct threats motivate splitting read from write:

1. App-initiated escalation — the app silently persists a dangerous value.
   Guarded by `appWritable`: the scope-writer (siy.10) must be structurally
   unable to persist a key that is not app-writable. Enforced by the mechanism,
   NOT by developer convention.

2. Untrusted-data escalation — an opened folder/document already contains a
   dangerous value (e.g. a cloned repo's .macdown.yml). Merely previewing it
   would silently widen access. Guarded on the READ side by a directional
   asymmetry: a security-flagged capability may be TIGHTENED by a layer
   silently, but LOOSENED beyond the app default only with explicit user
   consent — prompt once (Allow once / Allow for this folder / Keep default),
   storing the grant as app-side state keyed by folder path, NEVER written back
   into the (possibly untrusted) source file.

Categorization change from the original plan: htmlAssetLocalAccessScope moves
from app-only to OVERRIDABLE (readableFrom = app+folder+document) because it
governs how the document's own content resolves and renders. But:
  appWritable = explicit-only  (never auto-persisted)
  security    = yes            (loosening requires read-side consent)

Encode all three attributes in the schema so the resolver/reader/writer enforce
the invariants mechanically rather than by remembered rule.
acceptance_criteria: Schema enumerates every setting with readableFrom, appWritable, and security attributes. htmlAssetLocalAccessScope is readableFrom=app+folder+document, appWritable=explicit-only, security=yes. Security-sensitive keys are identifiable so the reader can gate loosening and the writer can refuse non-app-writable persistence.
notes:
FINALIZED CATEGORIZATION (2026-06-17):

OVERRIDABLE (readableFrom = app+folder+document; appWritable=yes; security=no) — the rendering/markdown/output surface:
- extension flags (11): extensionIntraEmphasis, extensionTables, extensionFencedCode, extensionAutolink, extensionStrikethough, extensionUnderline, extensionSuperscript, extensionHighlight, extensionFootnotes, extensionQuote, extensionSmartyPants
- markdownManualRender
- htmlStyleName, htmlHighlightingThemeName, htmlSyntaxHighlighting, htmlLineNumbers, htmlTaskList, htmlHardWrap, htmlMathJax, htmlMathJaxInlineDollar, htmlGraphviz, htmlMermaid, htmlCodeBlockAccessory, htmlDetectFrontMatter, htmlRendersTOC
- previewZoomRelativeToBaseFontSize
- (NEW, from siy.5) asset-handling policy: Link/Copy/Embed

OVERRIDABLE-SECURITY (readableFrom = app+folder+document; appWritable=explicit-only; security=yes):
- htmlAssetLocalAccessScope  (loosening requires read-side consent; never auto-persisted)

APP-ONLY (readableFrom = app):
- ALL editor* settings, including editorStyleName (editor theme affects the author's environment, not output)
- htmlDefaultDirectoryUrl
- firstVersionInstalled, latestVersionInstalled, updateIncludesPreReleases, supressesUntitledDocumentOnLaunch, createFileForLinkTarget, filesToOpen, pipedContentFileToOpen

DEFERRED/REVISIT: printMarginTop/Left/Bottom/Right are app-only for now but are output-affecting (PDF/print) and are candidates for document-overridable later — revisit alongside the print/PDF work.
close_reason: Closed

title: Layered settings cascade & image asset handling
description: Roadmap unifying image asset handling with a layered settings cascade. Three layers resolve general->specific: app defaults (MPPreferences) -> nested .macdown.yml directory configs (bounded by root:true) -> document front matter; last-writer-wins. One resolver (effectiveValue) and one shared overridable-settings schema underpin everything; security-sensitive keys (e.g. htmlAssetLocalAccessScope) are app-only. Image drag-and-drop is the first consumer: relative-path insertion for in-scope images, copy-to-assets, data: fallback, plus rendering fixes. Design discussion 2026-06-17.
